category_title: Elementary EMLLs exhibiting externalizing behaviors, restlessness, and non-compliance due to language barriers
minimal_context: Shen is in the regular classroom and is expected to complete his assigned work for today.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Shen, it is time to complete the assigned work for today.

STUDENT: (Remains quiet and looks around to follow the other students' actions, taking out his materials but not starting the actual assignment.)

TUTOR: You need to start writing on your paper.

STUDENT: (Becomes antsy in his seat, shifting around without making any progress on the assigned work.)

TUTOR: Please focus and do your work.

STUDENT: (Gets up out of his seat and begins to walk around the classroom without permission.) I don’t understand, so I won’t do it.

options.A: Establish goals for the assigned work through a joint process between Shen and the teacher based on his previous assessment results.
options.B: Blur boundaries between subjects to focus on integrated themes and co-construct a shared sense of morality during the assigned work.
options.C: Develop a behavior rubric in Mandarin and implement a rewards-based system where Shen earns stickers for meeting standards to exchange for a prize.
options.D: Make oneself available without forcing interaction, first simply sitting near Shen and then speaking quietly and gently touching him on the shoulder.
